equations.raw: We want (i=j, j'=0): L_{jk} R_{l,0} Psi_{kl} picks out k=0, l=j

equations.raw: So L[j,0] * R[j,0] = 1 -> gives the entry at (j,0)

equations.raw: X.R at position (j, 0) = sum_l Psi_{jl} R_{l0}
equations.context: So L[j,0] * R[j,0] = 1 -> gives the entry at (j,0) But R here acts as column multiplier: X -> X . R

equations.raw: D_F = sum_j |e_j><e_0| otimes |e_0><e_j| + |e_0><e_j| otimes |e_j><e_0|
equations.context: The tensor product decomposition is:

equations.raw: Wait. RIGHT action: X -> X . R. Entry (i, j') of X.R = sum_l X_{il} R_{lj'}
equations.context: R0j = np.zeros((4, 4), dtype=complex) R0j[0, j] = 1.0  |e_0><e_j| acting on right: X . |e_0><e_j| keeps col j, puts in col 0

equations.raw: For R = |e_0><e_j|: R_{lj'} = delta_{l0} delta_{j'j}

equations.context: Since H_i = L_Hi otimes I and R_{Ej0} commutes with I:
L_{Ej0} R_{Ej0} is NOT the tensor product L otimes R in the usual sense. The bimodule action X -> L.X.R has:

equations.raw: And H_i acts as: H_i(X) = L_Hi . X . I = L_Hi . X
So [D_F, H_i](X) = D_F(L_Hi . X) - L_Hi . D_F(X)
equations.context: And H_i acts as: H_i(X) = L_Hi . X . I = L_Hi . X

equations.raw: = sum_j E_{j0}(L_Hi X)E_{j0} + E_{0j}(L_Hi X)E_{0j}
equations.context: And H_i acts as: H_i(X) = L_Hi . X . I = L_Hi . X So [D_F, H_i](X) = D_F(L_Hi . X) - L_Hi . D_F(X)

equations.raw: = sum_j [E_{j0} L_Hi - L_Hi E_{j0}] X E_{j0}
equations.context: = sum_j E_{j0}(L_Hi X)E_{j0} + E_{0j}(L_Hi X)E_{0j} - L_Hi [sum_j E_{j0} X E_{j0} + E_{0j} X E_{0j}]

equations.raw: = sum_j [E_{j0}, L_Hi] . X . E_{j0}
equations.context: = sum_j [E_{j0} L_Hi - L_Hi E_{j0}] X E_{j0} + sum_j [E_{0j} L_Hi - L_Hi E_{0j}] X E_{0j}

equations.raw: Then for o(H_i) = H_i^dag (which on Psi_+ is L_Hi^dag):
equations.context: + sum_j [E_{0j}, L_Hi] . X . E_{0j}

equations.raw: [[D_F, H_i], o(H_i)](X) = [D_F, H_i](L_Hi^dag X) - L_Hi^dag [D_F, H_i](X)
equations.context: Then for o(H_i) = H_i^dag (which on Psi_+ is L_Hi^dag):

equations.context: Then for o(H_i) = H_i^dag (which on Psi_+ is L_Hi^dag): [[D_F, H_i], o(H_i)](X) = [D_F, H_i](L_Hi^dag X) - L_Hi^dag [D_F, H_i](X)
